enriched_points.expanded_content: Lithium-ion batteries exhibit energy densities of 100-265 Wh/kg, substantially higher than supercapacitors' 6-10 Wh/kg, making them more suitable for long-duration applications.
Supercapacitors offer superior cycle life, lasting over a million cycles, while lithium-ion batteries typically endure 500-2000 cycles, affecting their longevity in various applications.
Lithium-ion batteries are ideal for electric vehicles and renewable energy storage, while supercapacitors excel in applications requiring rapid charge/discharge, such as in micro-grids.
Key performance metrics include energy density, power density, cycle efficiency, and self-discharge rates, which vary significantly across electrochemical storage technologies.
Lithium-ion technology continues to innovate with enhanced materials, while supercapacitors are advancing towards integration with renewable sources, expanding their usage in grid applications.
